title: pleopeltis papyracea
intro: Pleopeltis papyracea, also known as the paper brake or papery polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, where its medicinal properties are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Pleopeltis papyracea include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal and anti-cancer agent. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically to treat wounds and skin conditions.
